encounter.reason: New nephrology consult for CKD stage 3b with albuminuria
encounter.location: Rio Grande Nephrology Associates, Albuquerque, New Mexico

labs.name: urine sediment microscopic assessment
labs.value: bland urine sediment / no active sediment

conditions.name: chronic kidney disease stage 3b due to diabetic kidney disease and hypertensive kidney disease
conditions.what_changed: Nephrology specialty evaluation established care, confirmed stage 3b chronic kidney disease as stable for outpatient management, documented diabetic and hypertensive etiology, and documented no indication for biopsy or acute nephrologic workup.

conditions.name: persistent albuminuria
conditions.what_changed: Persistence documented with urine albumin-creatinine ratio 286 mg/g; continued kidney-protective therapy affirmed.

conditions.name: hypertension
conditions.what_changed: Blood pressure documented as reasonably controlled in clinic and by home readings, with ongoing home BP log review plan.

conditions.name: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction / cardiorenal physiology affecting renal hemodynamics
conditions.what_changed: Renal dysfunction explicitly interpreted in the context of recent heart-failure hospitalization and ongoing diuretic therapy; continued monitoring of creatinine, potassium, and volume status planned.

conditions.name: chronic kidney disease mineral and bone disorder with mild secondary hyperparathyroidism and vitamin D insufficiency
mild anemia of chronic kidney disease / chronic disease pattern
conditions.what_changed: Mild anemia documented with hemoglobin 11.6 g/dL, ferritin 118 ng/mL, and transferrin saturation 24%; no acute intervention required.

medications.name: sacubitril/valsartan
medications.sig: continue current regimen for renal and cardiac benefit
medications.reason: chronic kidney disease with albuminuria; heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

medications.name: empagliflozin
medications.sig: continue current regimen for renal and cardiac benefit
medications.reason: type 2 diabetes mellitus; chronic kidney disease with albuminuria; heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

medications.name: diuretic therapy
medications.sig: continue existing heart-failure diuretic regimen managed with other clinicians
medications.reason: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction / volume management

document_references.name: Progress note / nephrology consultation note
document_references.description: New nephrology consultation documenting establishment of care for chronic kidney disease stage 3b with persistent albuminuria after recent heart-failure hospitalization. The note summarizes renal risk factors from diabetes and hypertension, reviewed renal and anemia/mineral bone disease labs, continued kidney-protective medications, documented no indication for biopsy or acute workup, and outlined follow-up monitoring.

document_references.name: Patient instructions / CKD education handout and medication safety review
document_references.description: Education document covering chronic kidney disease self-management, importance of blood pressure and diabetes control, and coordination with cardiology while on diuretic therapy. Includes nephrotoxin avoidance instructions, specifically avoiding over-the-counter NSAIDs and notifying clinicians before contrast exposure, plus instructions to keep a home BP log and obtain repeat labs before follow-up.

diagnostic_reports.type: Chronic kidney disease / renal monitoring laboratory report

diagnostic_reports.description: Ordered for follow-up monitoring before the next nephrology visit; no repeat results available yet.
Ordered for follow-up monitoring of persistent albuminuria before the next nephrology visit; no repeat results available yet.
